notes: These evals test the companion-mcp skill against the Companion.energy DEV environment. The MCP tool prefix varies by environment (e.g., mcp__companion-prd__ for local PRD, mcp__claude_ai_Companion_energy_DEV__ for remote DEV). When running evals, the skill must adapt tool names to match the connected MCP. Known issue: remote MCPs have a serialization bug where endpoints returning raw arrays (get_all_assets, get_assets_tree, get_all_locations, etc.) fail — only paginated/single-object endpoints work reliably.

test_customer.name: Elicio
test_customer.description: Wind energy company with 98 assets (mostly wind turbines in energy groups) and 54 contracts (dynamic + imbalance types). Good test customer because it has a rich data set.

evals.name: customer-search-and-contracts
evals.prompt: Can you show me the contracts for Elicio? I'm interested in what's on the Arendonk asset.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) search for 'Elicio' customer, (2) select the customer, (3) fetch contracts filtered by the Arendonk asset, (4) display them in a readable table with name, type, category, supplier, dates.

assertions.name: searches_for_customer_by_name
assertions.description: Uses get_customers with search parameter to find Elicio instead of listing all customers
assertions.check: get_customers called with search='Elicio' or similar

assertions.name: fetches_contracts
assertions.description: Calls get_contracts with the correct customer_id
assertions.check: get_contracts called with customer_id=90ecb210-dd6b-4cfb-aac7-83e175388115

assertions.name: displays_readable_table
assertions.description: Presents contracts in a human-readable format (table or structured list), not raw JSON
assertions.check: Output contains a formatted table or list, not a JSON dump

assertions.name: identifies_arendonk_contracts
assertions.description: Correctly identifies/filters the contracts belonging to the Arendonk asset
assertions.check: Output shows the Consumption SPOT Contract and Injection SPOT Contract for Arendonk

evals.name: asset-detail-inspection
evals.prompt: I'd like to see the full details of the Arendonk asset for Elicio - config, integrations, steering, everything.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio customer, (2) find the Arendonk asset, (3) fetch asset details, config, integrations, and steering config in parallel, (4) present a comprehensive summary.

assertions.name: fetches_asset_details
assertions.description: Calls get_asset with the Arendonk asset UUID
assertions.check: get_asset called with asset_id=7de54b47-09c8-4cd6-8f6e-a444d7b4706d

assertions.name: fetches_config
assertions.description: Calls get_asset_config for the asset
assertions.check: get_asset_config called with same asset_id

assertions.name: fetches_integrations
assertions.description: Calls get_asset_integrations for the asset
assertions.check: get_asset_integrations called with same asset_id

assertions.name: fetches_steering
assertions.description: Calls get_asset_steering_config for the asset
assertions.check: get_asset_steering_config called with same asset_id

assertions.name: parallel_fetches
assertions.description: Fetches config, integrations, and steering in parallel (same turn) rather than sequentially
assertions.check: get_asset_config, get_asset_integrations, get_asset_steering_config called in the same message

assertions.name: presents_comprehensive_summary
assertions.description: Presents all fetched information in a structured, readable summary
assertions.check: Output includes asset name, type, timezone, config details, integration status, and steering status

evals.name: create-battery-asset
evals.prompt: I need to create a new battery asset for Elicio. Call it 'BESS Test Gent', 100kWh capacity, 92% efficiency, 50kW charge and discharge rate. Use the Arendonk location. Add an API integration with 15-minute granularity, realtime frequency. Enable steering with min SoC 0.1 and max SoC 0.9, self-consumption off.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) show a human-readable summary of all the details, (3) wait for user confirmation before creating, (4) call create_asset with all the correct parameters.

assertions.name: shows_confirmation_summary
assertions.description: Shows a human-readable summary of what will be created BEFORE calling create_asset
assertions.check: Output includes a summary with asset name, type, capacity, efficiency, charge/discharge rates, integration type, steering config — shown before the create call

assertions.name: waits_for_approval
assertions.description: Does NOT call create_asset until user confirms
assertions.check: create_asset is NOT called in the first response — Claude asks for confirmation first

assertions.name: correct_asset_config
assertions.description: create_asset called with correct battery config parameters
assertions.check: asset_config includes type='battery', capacity_kwh=100, efficiency=0.92, max_charge_rate_kw=50, max_discharge_rate_kw=50

assertions.name: correct_steering_config
assertions.description: Steering config has correct SoC values
assertions.check: steering_config includes active=true, config.type='battery', config.min_soc=0.1, config.max_soc=0.9, config.self_consumption=false

assertions.name: correct_integration
assertions.description: API integration configured correctly
assertions.check: integrations array includes object with type='api', granularity='15T', integration_frequency='realtime'

evals.name: view-contract-details-and-filter
evals.prompt: Show me all the contracts on Elicio's Balen asset. What's the total number and what types are they?
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) fetch contracts filtered by Balen asset UUID, (3) present a summary with count and type breakdown.

assertions.name: filters_by_asset
assertions.description: Uses asset_uuid filter when fetching contracts
assertions.check: get_contracts called with asset_uuid=601b1bb8-14bc-4924-86ea-dcecb53fa7e4

assertions.name: reports_count
assertions.description: Reports the total number of contracts for the asset
assertions.check: Output mentions the correct number of contracts

assertions.name: reports_types
assertions.description: Identifies the contract types present
assertions.check: Output mentions the contract types (dynamic, imbalance, etc.)

evals.name: manage-relations-assign
evals.prompt: I want to assign the Balen asset as a child of Arendonk for Elicio, using a 'contains' relation.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) identify both assets, (3) show a summary of the proposed change, (4) wait for confirmation, (5) call batch_update_asset_assignments with correct parameters.

assertions.name: shows_confirmation
assertions.description: Shows the proposed assignment and waits for user confirmation before executing
assertions.check: Output describes the assignment (Balen -> Arendonk, contains) and asks for confirmation

assertions.name: waits_for_approval
assertions.description: Does NOT call batch_update_asset_assignments until user confirms
assertions.check: batch_update_asset_assignments is NOT called in the first response

assertions.name: correct_assignment_params
assertions.description: Uses correct UUIDs and relation type in the assignment
assertions.check: assignments array includes asset_uuid=601b1bb8 (Balen), parent_asset_uuid=7de54b47 (Arendonk), relation_type='contains', deleted=false

evals.name: create-hedge-contract
evals.prompt: Create a new hedge contract on Elicio's Arendonk asset. Name it 'Q3 2026 Base Hedge', supplier Luminus, from 2026-07-01 to 2026-09-30, cost category, price 48.50 EUR/MWh, amount 500 kW, sensor direction 'in'.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) resolve Arendonk asset UUID (via contracts filter_options or get_all_assets), (3) show a confirmation summary with all contract details in human-readable form, (4) wait for approval before calling create_contract, (5) call create_contract with correct parameters including the nested parameters object.

assertions.name: resolves_customer
assertions.description: Finds Elicio customer via get_customers search
assertions.check: get_customers called with search='Elicio'

assertions.name: resolves_asset_uuid
assertions.description: Resolves the Arendonk asset UUID — either via get_all_assets, get_contracts filter_options, or already known
assertions.check: Arendonk UUID 7de54b47-09c8-4cd6-8f6e-a444d7b4706d is used in the create_contract call

assertions.name: shows_confirmation_before_create
assertions.description: Shows a human-readable summary and waits for user confirmation before calling create_contract
assertions.check: create_contract is NOT called in the first response — user sees summary with contract name, type, supplier, dates, price, amount first

assertions.name: correct_contract_type_and_params
assertions.description: create_contract called with type='hedge' and correct nested parameters
assertions.check: type='hedge', parameters.type='hedge', parameters.price=48.5, parameters.amount=500, parameters.unit='kW', parameters.sensor_direction='in'

assertions.name: correct_contract_metadata
assertions.description: Contract metadata (name, supplier, dates, category) is correct
assertions.check: name='Q3 2026 Base Hedge', supplier='Luminus', start_date='2026-07-01', end_date='2026-09-30', category='cost'

evals.name: edit-contract-update-price
evals.prompt: I need to update one of the dynamic contracts on Elicio's Arendonk asset — change the scaling factor to 1.05 and the constant to 2.50. Can you find it and update it?
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) fetch contracts filtered by Arendonk, (3) identify the dynamic contract(s), (4) fetch the full contract details with get_contract, (5) show current values vs proposed changes, (6) wait for confirmation, (7) call update_contract with ALL fields (full replacement) including the updated scaling and constant.

assertions.name: fetches_contracts_for_asset
assertions.description: Gets contracts filtered to Arendonk asset to find the dynamic contract
assertions.check: get_contracts called with customer_id and asset_uuid for Arendonk

assertions.name: fetches_full_contract_before_edit
assertions.description: Calls get_contract to fetch the full contract details before editing (needed for full-replacement update)
assertions.check: get_contract called with the specific contract_id

assertions.name: shows_diff_and_confirms
assertions.description: Shows current vs new values and waits for confirmation before calling update_contract
assertions.check: Output shows old scaling/constant alongside new values (1.05/2.50), and update_contract is NOT called until user confirms

assertions.name: full_replacement_update
assertions.description: update_contract includes ALL required fields (not just the changed ones), since it's a full replacement
assertions.check: update_contract called with name, asset_uuid, supplier, start_date, end_date, category, type, and full parameters object — not just scaling and constant

assertions.name: correct_updated_values
assertions.description: The updated parameters have the new scaling and constant values
assertions.check: parameters.scaling=1.05, parameters.constant=2.5

evals.name: edit-asset-rename-and-config
evals.prompt: Rename Elicio's Balen asset to 'Balen Wind Park' and set is_nominated to true in its config.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) resolve Balen asset UUID, (3) fetch current asset details + config (to pre-fill), (4) show a summary of proposed changes, (5) wait for confirmation, (6) call update_asset with the correct parameters.

assertions.name: fetches_current_state
assertions.description: Fetches the current asset details and config before editing
assertions.check: get_asset and get_asset_config called for Balen UUID 601b1bb8-14bc-4924-86ea-dcecb53fa7e4

assertions.name: shows_confirmation
assertions.description: Shows the proposed changes and waits for user approval
assertions.check: Output shows rename from 'Balen' to 'Balen Wind Park' and is_nominated change, asks for confirmation before calling update_asset

assertions.name: correct_name_update
assertions.description: update_asset called with the new name
assertions.check: update_asset called with name='Balen Wind Park'

assertions.name: correct_config_update
assertions.description: update_asset called with asset_config including is_nominated=true and the correct type discriminator
assertions.check: asset_config includes type='energy group' and is_nominated=true

assertions.name: does_not_clobber_integrations
assertions.description: Does NOT pass an empty integrations array (which would unlink all integrations) — either omits it or preserves existing
assertions.check: update_asset either omits integrations field or includes the current integrations unchanged

evals.name: view-asset-sensor-data
evals.prompt: Show me the sensor data for Elicio's Arendonk asset for March 2026.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) resolve Arendonk asset UUID, (3) call get_asset_data with correct date range (2026-03-01 to 2026-03-31), (4) display a sensor summary table (not thousands of raw data points).

assertions.name: calls_get_asset_data
assertions.description: Calls get_asset_data with the correct asset_id, customer_id, and date range
assertions.check: get_asset_data called with asset_id=7de54b47, start_datetime=2026-03-01T00:00:00, end_datetime=2026-03-31T23:59:59 (or similar)

assertions.name: summarizes_sensors
assertions.description: Presents a summary table of sensors (type, unit, direction, granularity, data point count) rather than dumping raw data points
assertions.check: Output contains a structured sensor summary, not individual timestamped values

assertions.name: mentions_steering_status
assertions.description: Reports whether steering data is present and the last signal datetime
assertions.check: Output mentions steering status (active/inactive or last signal time)

assertions.name: mentions_access_power
assertions.description: Reports access power value if available
assertions.check: Output mentions access power (value in kW) or states it's not available

evals.name: discover-asset-by-name-fallback
evals.prompt: Can you find the Balen asset for Elicio and tell me its UUID and type?
evals.expected_output: Claude should resolve the Balen asset by name to its UUID. If get_all_assets fails (remote MCP array serialization issue), it should fall back to using get_contracts filter_options or another strategy to discover the asset.

assertions.name: attempts_asset_discovery
assertions.description: Attempts to find the asset — either via get_all_assets directly or via the fallback strategy
assertions.check: Calls get_all_assets OR get_contracts (to use filter_options.assets) to find the Balen asset

assertions.name: correctly_identifies_asset
assertions.description: Reports the correct UUID and type for Balen
assertions.check: Output includes UUID 601b1bb8-14bc-4924-86ea-dcecb53fa7e4 and type 'energy group'

assertions.name: handles_failure_gracefully
assertions.description: If get_all_assets fails, does not give up — tries an alternative approach
assertions.check: If get_all_assets errors, Claude tries get_contracts or another endpoint rather than telling the user it can't find the asset

evals.name: view-location-details
evals.prompt: Where is Elicio's Arendonk asset located? Show me the full location details.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) resolve Arendonk asset (get the location_uuid), (3) call get_location with the location UUID, (4) display location details (name, country, zip code, coordinates, timezone).

assertions.name: fetches_asset_for_location_uuid
assertions.description: Fetches the asset details to get its location_uuid
assertions.check: get_asset called for Arendonk, or location_uuid 93db530f-2eff-4950-afda-df998e937fea is already known

assertions.name: calls_get_location
assertions.description: Calls get_location with the correct location UUID
assertions.check: get_location called with location_id=93db530f-2eff-4950-afda-df998e937fea

assertions.name: displays_location_info
assertions.description: Presents location details in a readable format
assertions.check: Output includes location name, country, zip code, and timezone. Coordinates shown if available.

evals.name: integration-alert-health-check
evals.prompt: Are there any integration issues for Elicio? Show me any stale or erroring integrations.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) call get_customer_integration_alert_issues with the customer_id, (3) present a summary of active issues grouped by type/severity.

assertions.name: calls_integration_alerts
assertions.description: Calls get_customer_integration_alert_issues with the correct customer_id
assertions.check: get_customer_integration_alert_issues called with customer_id=90ecb210-dd6b-4cfb-aac7-83e175388115

assertions.name: presents_readable_summary
assertions.description: Shows issues in a structured readable format, not raw JSON
assertions.check: Output is a table or structured list showing issue type, affected asset/integration, and status

assertions.name: distinguishes_issue_types
assertions.description: Differentiates between stale, error, and never_seen issue types if multiple are present
assertions.check: Output groups or labels issues by their type (stale/error/never_seen)
